message:
refactor: standardize FCP protocol loop, migrate test runtimes to block_on_sync, and multiple fixes

Protocol loop: replace line.is_empty() with should_skip_protocol_line
(whitespace-tolerant), extract parse_error_response with JSON-RPC 2.0
envelope, add generic run_fcp_loop_with_io for testability across 12
connectors. Telegram switches to persistent multi-thread runtime.

Test runtimes: use OnceLock for Tokio compat handle in fcp-async-core
ensuring single driver thread. Migrate 11+ test files from manual
Builder to block_on_sync one-liner.

Fixes:
- Add HTTP 408 to retryable status codes (linear, pinecone)
- Redact sensitive headers in GraphqlClientConfig Debug output
- Replace signal-hook with native async signals in fcp-host
- Prevent division-by-zero in RaptorQ encoder for zero symbol/chunk size
- Fix RetryPolicy off-by-one: max_attempts counts total including initial
- Enforce strict W3C Trace Context validation (lowercase, length, non-zero)

Google connectors: rustfmt import and closure formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3012,8 +3012,6 @@ dependencies = [
  "semver",
  "serde",
  "serde_json",
- "signal-hook 0.3.18",
- "signal-hook-tokio",
  "tempfile",
  "thiserror 2.0.18",
  "tower",
@@ -9118,18 +9116,6 @@ dependencies = [
  "libc",
 ]
 
-[[package]]
-name = "signal-hook-tokio"
-version = "0.3.1"
-source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
-checksum = "213241f76fb1e37e27de3b6aa1b068a2c333233b59cca6634f634b80a27ecf1e"
-dependencies = [
- "futures-core",
- "libc",
- "signal-hook 0.3.18",
- "tokio",
-]
-
 [[package]]
 name = "signature"
 version = "2.2.0"

@@ -1171,11 +1171,7 @@ mod tests {
     where
         F: std::future::Future<Output = T>,
     {
-        fcp_async_core::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()
-            .enable_all()
-            .build()
-            .expect("build test runtime")
-            .block_on(future)
+        fcp_async_core::runtime::block_on_sync(future).expect("build sync test runtime")
     }
 
     #[test]
